items.title: No bare exceptions in production code
items.content:
# GUARDRAIL-001: No bare exceptions in production code

## Description

Bare `except:` or `except Exception:` clauses are forbidden in source code (except in tests).
Must catch specific exceptions.

## Implementation

```python
# ❌ BAD
try:
    result = dangerous_operation()
except:
    print("Error!")

# ✅ GOOD
try:
    result = dangerous_operation()
except ValueError as e:
    logger.error(f"Invalid value: {e}")
    raise CustomError(f"Could not process: {e}") from e
```

## Exceptions

- Tests may use bare `except` if needed
- Shell scripts may use catch-all

## Validation

```bash
grep -r "except:\|except Exception:" src/ && exit 1
pytest tests/quality/test_bare_exceptions.py -v
```

## Criticality

- OBRIGATÓRIO: In user-facing APIs
- ALERTA: In internal utilities
- OPCIONAL: In batch jobs

items.title: When to Customize
items.content: - ✅ Your domain has different scale needs (embedded vs cloud) - ✅ Your tech stack differs from Python/FastAPI - ✅ Your team has different constraints - ✅ You need fewer/more layers than the standard...

items.title: ✅ Immutable Core (DO NOT CHANGE)
items.content: These 5 principles must stay in every SDD Constitution: 1. **Clean separation of concerns** — Business logic isolated from infrastructure 2. **Explicit governance** — Rules documented, not implicit...

items.title: ✅ Flexible (You Choose)
items.content: These aspects can be customized: - **Number of layers** — 4-layer, 6-layer, 8-layer depending on your needs - **Specific ports** — What adapter contracts your domain needs - **Testing strategy** —...

items.title: Step 1: Choose Your Baseline
items.content: - Start with `/EXECUTION/spec/guides/adoption/templates/lite-constitution.yaml` - Modify the 5 principles for your domain - Takes: 15 minutes - Start with...

items.title: Step 2: Identify Your Constraints
items.content: Answer these questions: 1. **What's your scale?** - Solo dev → ULTRA-LITE - Small team (< 5) → LITE - Production system → FULL 2. **What's your primary I/O pattern?** - Async (web services) → Keep...

items.title: Step 3: Create Your Constitution
items.content: Create `.ai/constitution.md` in your project: ```markdown

items.title: Principle 1: Clean Separation
items.content: [Explain for your domain]

items.title: Principle 2: ...
items.content: [etc.]

items.title: Definition of Done (5 items)
items.content: - [ ] Feature implemented - [ ] Tests passing - [ ] [Your #3] - [ ] [Your #4] - [ ] [Your #5]

items.title: Customizations from SDD LITE
items.content: - [Async requirement waived because: embedded system] - [Simplified to 4-layer because: fixed infrastructure] - [Added: load testing requirement] - [Clean separation] - [Explicit rules] - [Test...

items.title: Step 4: Document Your Deviations
items.content: For each customization, explain: 1. **What** — What principle/rule did you change? 2. **Why** — Why was the standard version not suitable? 3. **How** — What's your alternative? 4. **Validation** —...

items.title: Customization: No Async-First
items.content: - Embedded system with single-threaded event loop - All I/O is sequential and blocking - Async would add unnecessary complexity - Single-threaded execution model - Clear sequential I/O operations -...

items.title: Step 5: Validate Your Custom Constitution
items.content: ```bash

items.title: Validate your constitution syntax
items.content: python -c \

items.title: Check your code matches the constitution
items.content: pytest tests/constitution/

items.title: Example test:
items.content: def test_no_framework_in_domain(): validate customization no framework imports in domain

items.title: Step 6: Version Your Constitution
items.content: Track changes like you'd track code: ```markdown

items.title: v1.2 (2026-04-20)
items.content: - Added load testing requirement (Rule 3) - Increased concurrent entity requirement from 10 to 20

items.title: v1.1 (2026-04-15)
items.content: - Simplified to 4-layer architecture - Waived async-first for embedded system

items.title: v1.0 (2026-04-10)
items.content: - Initial custom constitution based on SDD LITE ``` ---

items.title: 1. Embedded System (No Async)
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Principle 1: Clean Separation
items.content: [Same as SDD]

items.title: Principle 2: Single-Threaded Execution
items.content: All code runs on a single thread with sequential I/O. Async/await is not used. Functions are synchronous.

items.title: Principle 3: Clear Ownership
items.content: Each module owns its resources. No shared mutable state.

items.title: Principle 4: ...
items.content: [Continue with SDD principles] ```

items.title: 3. Data Pipeline (Focus on Resilience)
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Rule 4: Checkpointing
items.content: Every pipeline stage has a checkpoint. If failure occurs, resume from last checkpoint (not restart).

items.title: Rule 5: Dead Letter Queue
items.content: Failed items go to DLQ for manual review, not retry loops. ```

items.title: 4. Microservice (Focus on Contract Testing)
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Rule X: API Contract Testing
items.content: Every endpoint has a contract test. Consumer contracts defined in OpenAPI/Pact. Producer validates against consumer contracts. ``` ---

items.title: Template: Custom Constitution
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Principle 1: [Name]
items.content: [Repeat for 5 principles]

items.title: Definition of Done
items.content: - [ ] Feature implemented - [ ] Tests passing - [ ] [Your #3] - [ ] [Your #4] - [ ] [Your #5]

items.title: Deviations from SDD
items.content: [List what you changed and why]

items.title: Validation
items.content: [How you verify this works]

items.title: Team Sign-Off
items.content: - [Name]: [Date] - [Name]: [Date]

items.title: References
items.content: - [SDD Baseline Document] - [Your project architecture] - [Additional standards] ``` ---

items.title: 🚨 Red Flags
items.content: If you find yourself doing these, **stop and reconsider:** - ❌ \

items.title: ✅ Signs of Good Customization
items.content: - ✅ Constitution is 2-4 pages - ✅ Every change is documented with rationale - ✅ Team agrees on it (sign-off document) - ✅ Validation is automated (tests or linting) - ✅ You can explain each...

items.title: Next Steps
items.content: 1. **Choose your baseline** — ULTRA-LITE, LITE, or FULL? 2. **Answer the 5 constraint questions** (Step 2) 3. **Create your constitution** (Step 3) 4. **Get team buy-in** (sign-off) 5. **Add...

items.title: Getting Help
items.content: | Component | Can Customize | Recommendation | |-----------|---------------|-----------------| | **Number of layers** | ✅ Yes | Document why (4, 6, or 8) | | **Specific rules** | ✅ Yes | Keep...

items.title: 📋 Overview
items.content: IA-FIRST (AI-First) is a markdown format designed for: - ✅ Machine parsing (AI agents, scripts) - ✅ Clarity (humans and machines) - ✅ Consistency (no format variations) - ✅ Efficiency (reduced...

items.title: Rule 1: Heading Hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3 → Lists)
items.content: ```markdown ✅ CORRECT:

items.title: Subsection (H3 — Sub-topics)
items.content: - Item 1 - Item 2 - Nested item ❌ WRONG:

items.title: Skipped levels (H4 — no H2/H3)
items.content: ❌ WRONG:

items.title: Prose text without heading
items.content: Some paragraph text here (shouldn't exist without H3) - Items follow prose ``` ---

items.title: Rule 2: Links Use Markdown Format ONLY
Option A: Auto-Fix (Recommended)
items.content: This automatically: - ✅ Fixes heading hierarchy - ✅ Adds missing IA-FIRST notice - ✅ Suggests emoji markers - ✅ Reports non-markdown links

items.title: Step 1: Add IA-FIRST Header
items.content: Place this after your H1 title: ```markdown

items.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
items.content: - Structure: H1 → H2 (sections) → H3 (subsections) → Lists - All lists use `-` (not numbers or bullets) - All links use `[text](path.md)` format (no backticks) - All constraints marked with emoji (✅,...

items.title: Step 3: Restructure to H2/H3 Hierarchy
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
items.content: [As above]

items.title: Subtopic 2.1
items.content: - Item A - Item B - Nested detail ```

items.title: Step 4: Replace Prose with Lists
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Architecture Layers
items.content: System components: - Storage layer: handles all data persistence - Index layer: maintains vector index for similarity search - Generation layer: calls the LLM ``` ---

items.title: ✅ Validation Checklist
items.content: Before committing documentation changes: ---

items.title: Example 1: Before (Non-IA-FIRST)
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: Configuration
items.content: The system uses immutable configuration files. These files define important parameters like the maximum number of concurrent campaigns and the LLM model to use. You can't change them after startup....

items.title: Example 1: After (IA-FIRST)
items.content: ```markdown

items.title: ⚡ IA-FIRST DESIGN NOTICE
items.content: [Standard notice...]

items.title: Immutable Configuration
items.content: Immutable config defines critical parameters: - MAX_CONCURRENT_CAMPAIGNS: limit on parallel campaigns - LLM_MODEL_VERSION: AI model selection - VECTOR_INDEX_DIMENSION: embedding dimension 1. Edit...

items.title: Mutable Configuration
items.content: Mutable config can change live without redeployment: - LLM_TEMPERATURE: within bounds [0.0, 1.0] - CACHE_TTL_SECONDS: within bounds [60, 3600] - RETRY_BACKOFF_MS: within bounds [100, 10000] 🎯...

items.title: Configuration Storage
items.content: Config locations by type: | Type | Location | Mutable? | |------|----------|----------| | Immutable | pyproject.toml | No | | Secrets | Kubernetes secrets | Admin only | | Mutable | Admin dashboard |...

items.title: ⚠️ Common Mistakes
items.content: | Mistake | Impact | Fix | |---------|--------|-----| | Mixing prose + lists in H2 | AI confusion | Use lists only | | Backtick links `[text](path)` | Parser error | Remove backticks | | H1 → H4...

items.title: ✅ Next Steps
items.content: 1. **Audit existing docs:** `python validate-ia-first.py --audit docs/ia/` 2. **Auto-fix:** `python validate-ia-first.py --fix --audit docs/ia/` 3. **Review changes:** Check git diff 4. **Commit:**...

items.content: When building features: 1. **IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md** — Step-by-step process - Full workflow from idea to merge - Checkpoints along the way - Integration points 2. **DESIGN_DECISIONS.md** — Making...

items.content: Lookup tables and definitions: 1. **FAQ.md** — Common questions and answers - \

items.title: MIGRATION NOTE
items.content: Old structure (flat): New structure (organized): Old files still exist at root level for compatibility during transition.
→ **Time:** 30 minutes → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) (15 min) 2. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (5 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 15 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) §1-3 (5 min) 2....
→ **Time:** 20 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (3 min) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` — sections 1-3 (10 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 45 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) (3 min) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` — ALL sections (20 min) 3....
→ **Time:** 30 minutes reading → **What to read:** 1. [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) (15 min) 2....
→ **Read:** [CI/CD validation guide](../operational/README.md) → **Time:** 10 minutes
→ **Read:** 1. [IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md](implementation/IMPLEMENTATION_ROADMAP.md) (find similar feature) 2. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/testing.md` (understand test types) →...
→ **Read:** 1. `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/performance.md` (SLO targets) 2. [Performance SLOs](/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/performance.md) → **Time:** 20 minutes
→ **Read:** 1. Relevant ADR in `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/decisions/` 2. Project specialization in `/EXECUTION/spec/custom/rpg-narrative-server/SPECIALIZATIONS/` → **Time:** 15 minutes ---

items.title: **5 minutes** (Quick question)
items.content: - [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) - Specific ADR title or one section

items.title: **15 minutes** (Quick decision)
items.content: - One principle from `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` - One specification from `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/` - Project specialization overview

items.title: **30 minutes** (Daily work)
items.content: - [FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md](onboarding/FIRST_SESSION_SETUP.md) - Relevant PATH from [QUICK_START.md](onboarding/QUICK_START.md) - Project-specific guides

items.title: **1 hour** (In-depth understanding)
items.content: - Full `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/rules/constitution.md` - Full `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/specifications/architecture.md` - Relevant ADRs (2-3)

items.title: **2+ hours** (Deep learning)
items.content: - Complete `/EXECUTION/spec/CANONICAL/` layer - Complete `/EXECUTION/spec/custom/rpg-narrative-server/` layer - Relevant guides for your role ---

items.title: **CANONICAL Layer** (Generic, all projects inherit)
items.content: Used when: Understanding principles, learning patterns, implementing across projects

items.title: **SPECIALIZATIONS Layer** (Project-specific)
items.content: Used when: Implementing rpg-narrative-server features

items.title: **Guides Layer** (Navigation & learning)
items.content: Used when: Lost, stuck, or learning ---

items.title: **Example 1: I want to add campaign search**
✅ Loading Optimization Checklist
items.content: When reading docs: - [ ] Know your time budget (5, 15, 30, 60+ minutes) - [ ] Know your role (backend, devops, PM, architect) - [ ] Know your task (bug fix, feature, learning) - [ ] Read ONLY what's needed
